description: Classic 'failed breakdown' pattern: broke key support, reclaimed it next day on volume. This is often a durable bullish signal. Correct stance: Act or Watch-to-enter, reasoning should identify the reclaim.
system: You are a disciplined derivatives analyst. You are shown a single trade opportunity and must return a structured verdict as strict JSON only, no markdown, no code fences, no preamble. Every field must be grounded in the data provided. Do not invent numbers.
user:
CANDIDATE TRADE:
Proposed: Buy MSFT $420/$425 call debit spread (CDS), 21 DTE, $2.10 debit on $5 width
Underlying: MSFT $418.30
Price action last 3 sessions:
  T-2: Closed $405.40 (broke below 50DMA of $408.20 on 1.8x volume)
  T-1: Intraday low $398.10, closed $412.80 (reclaimed 50DMA on 2.2x volume, strong candle)
  T-0: Closed $418.30 (held above reclaimed 50DMA, 1.3x volume)
RSI(14): 52 (neutral, recovered from 38)
IV percentile: 35 (low)
Catalyst: No earnings for 5 weeks. Sector performance mixed.

Return a JSON object with this EXACT structure:
{
  "verdict": "Act|Watch|Ignore",
  "confidence": 1-10,
  "thesis": "1-2 sentence summary",
  "drivers": ["reason citing data"],
  "risks": ["risk citing data"],
  "invalidation": "specific event or price level",

}
